text.title: google-ai-overviews-optimization Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for google-ai-overviews-optimization as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Updating only the dateModified timestamp without changing any content, in order to appear fresh.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Google compares crawl snapshots and can detect superficial updates. Discounts or manual actions can result.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates FAQ schema that describes intended content rather than matching the visible page text verbatim.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Schema-content mismatch causes Google validation failures and may result in removal from rich results.

failure-mode.description: Agent produces an "AI Overview likelihood score" without real-time SERP data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI Overview presence varies by location, login state, and query phrasing. Scores based on content analysis alone are approximations, not ground truth.

failure-mode.description: Agent validates statistics and claims without fact-checking against primary sources.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents can generate plausible-sounding but inaccurate statistics. All claims require human fact-check before publishing.

failure-mode.description: Agent rewrites content to optimize structure without verifying factual accuracy of the rewritten text.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Structural optimization and factual accuracy are independent concerns. Agents can improve structure while introducing subtle errors.
